Cognate with Odia ବିଜୁଳି (bijuḷi), Gujarati વીજળી (vījaḷī), Nepali बिजुली (bijulī), Assamese বিজুলী (bizuli), Punjabi ਬਿਜਲੀ (bijlī), Sindhi بجلي (bjle).

ety: S. विद्युत्+dim. aff. lī = S. र
def: s.f. Lightning; thunderbolt ( = bajr)
more: n. f. S. विद्युत, Pr. vijjulī, vijjū . 1. Lightning; a thunderbolt ( bajjar ). 1. "Kāhe ko do nainā chamke, kāhe chamkī bīj?" "Chhailā ko do nainā chamke, barsan chamkī bīj, Ghūṅgaṭ meṅ do nainā chamke, bādar chamkī bīj." Song. "Whence is the light of those two eyes, and whence the lightning flash?" "For my gallant these two eyes shine, for rain's the lightning flash, Behind my veil these two eyes shine, within the cloud's the flash." 2. Bijlī chamke, mehā barse, larje māro jiarā, Purwā, pachhwā pawan chalat hai, kaise bārūṅ dīarā? Song. Ṭhumrī . Quick lightnings flash and rains pour down, my heart it beats with fright, From East and West it blows so hard, my lamp how shall I light? 3. Bahut dirab sanchaë jahāṅ, chor, Rāj bhaë hoë, Kāṅsī ūpar bījarī parat, kahat sab koë . Dohā. Where much wealth is the fear of thieves and kings prevails alway, So metals struck by lightning are, as all th…

s. f. 1. Lightning. 2. A thunderbolt: see بجر bajr.

def: v.n. To open, expand; to blow (as a flower), bloom; to be pleased or delighted, become radiant with joy; to smile, laugh
more: v. n. S. विकसन from वि + कस् to expand. 1. To blow or expand (a flower). Kalī bikasnā . 2. To smile; laugh; to be pleased, delighted ( bihasnā ). Kisī ko dukh ho, yeh khaṛe bikaste haiṅ . 3. To fade; droop; to be blighted; to rot; mortify. Kalejā pakaṛ māṅ to bas rah gaī, Kalī kī tarah se bikas rah gaī! Mīr Hasan. Her hand pressed 'gainst her heart, transfixed she stood, A blighted bud her fresh young maidenhood!
v. n. 1. To blow or expand (as a flower). 2. To be delighted, to smile.

Bin lalwā din bikaṭ kaṭat saë, Chhātī sāl gayā lalwā . W. Rus. Dirge. Without my child the day drags heavily along, My heart pierced through and through, my child from me is gone. bikaṭ pahāṛā , n. m. The table of squares.

def: v.n. To be scattered, strewn, spread, dispersed; to be sown broadcast; to be disordered, disarranged, tossed, dishevelled (as hair); to be wasted, spoiled, ruined; to change for the worse, become worse; to become angry or enraged
more: v. n. S. विकृ to scatter. 1. To be scattered, dispersed, spread, strewn, cast. Nāj bikharnā . 2. To be sown broadcast. Bīj bikharnā . 3. To be dropped, shed, split. Kauṛiyāṅ bikhar gaīn . 4. To be disordered, disarranged, jumbled, tumbled, tossed (hair). 1. Bāl haiṅ bikhre, band haiṅ ṭūṭe, ṭūṭā kān kā bālā hai, Ham ne to yāṅ tāṛ līyā, kuchh dāl meṅ kālā kālā hai . Jurat. Her hair all tossed, her bodice strings and earrings broken too, There's something in her porridge black, I'm sure I have the clue. 2. Bāl suljhānā terā kaṅghī se dil uljhāë hai, Aur bikhre dekh-kar, bas jī hī bikhrā jāë hai . Baqā. Plastered and combed your hair my heart has quite upset, Unkempt and tossed, my heart is tossed and overset. 5. To be wasted, spoilt, destroyed, thrown away. 6. To be calcined, burnt to cinder, pulverized; to crumble. 1. Chāṅdī bikhar gaī . 2. Laḍḍū bikhre jāte haiṅ . 7. To be done up, faint,…
v. n. 1. To be scattered, dispersed or dishevelled. 2. To become angry ( Hindawī ).

def: s.m. Hole (particularly of a rat or mouse); burrow
more: n. m. S. विल, Palī. bilam . A hole; a burrow. 1. Chūhā bil meṅ samātā hī nahīṅ, kānoṅ bāṅdhā chhāj . Prov. The rat wont fit in his hole, and he hangs two winnowing fans ( sūp ) to his ears! (He can hardly keep himself and he must have a dozen servants). 2. Chūhe kā bachchā bil hī khodtā hai . Prov. Saying. The mouse (like the mother-rat) burrows holes. bil ḍhūṅḍnā , v. n. To look for some hole to creep into ( chhupte phirnā ). Abhī nālish kar dūṅgā, to bil ḍhūṅḍtā phiregā . I have only to lodge a complaint and you'll be at your wit's end for some hole to creep into.
n. m. A bill of exchange; draft; cheque. bil jārī karnā ; H. hunḍī k . v. a. To draw a bill or cheque. G. G. bil sarkārī , n. m. A treasury draft. G. G.
subs: v.n. To look for a hole to creep into (through fear, &c.), to wish to escape or hide oneself.
wety: From Sanskrit बल (bala), from Proto-Indo-European *bel- (“power, strength”).

s.m. Car or chariot of the gods (sometimes serving as a seat or throne, and at others carrying them through the skies self-directed and self-moving); car or vehicle (generally); ornamental bier (on which a Hindū corpse is borne to the place of cremation).
more: n. m. S. विमान from विमा to traverse. 1. The chariot or vehicle of the gods, sometimes serving as a throne, and at others carrying them through the skies self-moving and self-directing. Ahran kī chorī karī, karā suī kā dān re, Ūṅche chaṛh-ke dekhan lāgā kītnī dūr bimān re . He steals an anvil and a needle gives in charity, He then the housetop mounts to see "how far's Heaven's chariot sent for me." 2. An ornamental bier on which the corpse of an old man is borne to the place of cremation. Hin.
s. m. A car or chariot of the gods (sometimes serving as a seat or throne, and at others carrying them through the skies self-directed and self-moving).

adv. S. बिना Without; exclusive of; except; save; minus; wanting. 1. Tājā ṭūk pakā de, mātā, bāsī maiṅ na khāūṅgā, Tājā khāë binā maiṅ nāhīṅ ḍhor charāwan jāūṅgā . Rus. Song. Fresh bread for me, bake mother, stale I will not eat, The cattle I'll not graze if fresh bread I dont eat. 2. Deh nain bin, rain chandr bin, dhartī megh binā, Jaise panḍit bed bin hīnā, taise pirānī Hari nām binā, Panchhī par bin, hasti dant bin, nārī purakh binā, Beswā-putr pitā bin hīnā, taise pirānī Hari nām binā, Kūp nīr bin, dhain khīr bin, mandar dīp biṇā, Jaise tarwar phal bin hīnā, taise pirānī Hari nām binā, Tū sumran kar le mere manā. Bhajan . Nānak Shāh. An eyeless frame, a moonless night, the earth rain showers without, As pandits void of learning, so the soul is Har without, Birds without wings, or tuskless elephant, maid man without, As harlot's son no father owns, the soul Har's name without, A well …

ety: S. विनाश; rt. नश् with वि
def: s.m. Disappearance, destruction, utter loss, ruin, death, annihilation
more: n. m. S. विनाश from वि+नश् to be ruined. Destruction; utter loss; ruin; annihilation. 1. Nadī kināre rūkhṛā jab tab hoë binās . Prov. Luxuriant trees upon the river side Must go some time or other with the tide. Prov. 2. Bāman mantrī, bhāṭ khavās, tā rājā kā hoë binās . A Brahman for prime minister, a bard for an attendant, the kingdom soon to ruin went. binās'nā, binās karnā , v. a. To destroy; ruin; annihilate. Phūl chaman kā nās kīyā tain, mevā sab hī bināsī, Jā, chal de, nikas, bhāg, kyoṅ āyā hai tū yāṅsī . Sah. Play. Chaubolā . You have destroyed the garden flowers and orchards too, Out hence, away, quick fly, what have you here to do?
subs: v.t. To wipe out, efface, ruin. spoil, destroy, annihilate.

def: s.f. Bowing down, bending low; humility, modesty; submission; apology; solicitation, entreaty, supplication, petition
more: n. f. S. विनति from विनम् to bend down. A bending or bowing low; petition; supplication; prayer; pressing solicitation; suit; entreaty; importunity. bintī karnā , v. a. To entreat; beseech; implore, etc. Phr.—To fall on one's knees; to throw oneself at the feet of. 1. Bintī karat hūṅ, paṛat torī paīyāṅ, Achchhe, saīyāṅ, chhāṛ de morī baīyāṅ . Song. Ṭhumrī . My husband, prostrate at your feet, Let go my arm, I do entreat. 2. Ṭhāḍi ghāṭ binatī karūṅ, Morī pār lagā de nāvaṛyā . Wom. Song. Ṭhumrī .
s. f. An apology, submission, solicitation.
subs: (-kī), To entreat (of), beseech, implore, supplicate, &c.; — bintī-kar, adv. Supplicatingly, beseechingly.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit विज्ञप्ति (vijñapti).

def: s.m. Cotton-seed (considered very fattening food for cattle: it is also burnt with oil as a light)
more: Mag. n. m. H. ban, bāṅgā cotton. Cotton seed given to cattle as fodder, and also burnt with oil as a light. 1. Gunḍe chale bazār, binaule ḍhāp rakhyo! Prov. The roughs are abroad, cover up your cotton seed. (They wont even spare such a trifling thing). 2. Binaule kī lūṭ meṅ barchhī kā ghāo! Prov. In the plunder of mere cotton seed, a spear-wound was his only meed. sūt ke binaule honā , v. n. To be spoiled or undone (a work which must be done over again).
s. m. The seed of the cotton tree. (It is said to be very fattening food for cattle). بنولاچابنا binaulā chābnā, To say unpleasant things.
